title: First Week Check-In
body:
Dear [Recruit Name],

We're thinking of you every single moment. By now you've arrived, been processed, and are starting to find your footing. We know it's overwhelming right now - the noise, the pace, the new faces - but you were built for this.

At home, everything feels a little quieter without you here. We keep the routines going just like you'd want.

We are so incredibly proud of you. You took a step that most people only talk about. That takes real courage.

Write back when you can. We check the mailbox every single day.

All our love,
[Your Name]

title: You've Got This
body:
Dear [Recruit Name],

There may be moments where you wonder if you can do this. You can. You absolutely can.

Think about every hard thing you've overcome before. You faced those moments and came out stronger. This is no different - it's just bigger. Every hard day in training is building the person we already know you are.

We don't talk about how hard the waiting is here at home - because our job is to keep things light for you. But know that every night we think of you, and every morning we start the day with you in our hearts.

Keep going. We'll be there cheering at graduation.

With so much love,
[Your Name]

title: News from Home
body:
Dear [Recruit Name],

Life at home keeps moving, but it's not quite the same without you here.

This week a lot has happened that I know you'd want to hear about. [Share a news update - a family event, something funny, a neighbor's news, a pet story.]

Everyone asks about you. They all send their love and want you to know they're proud.

The weather has been [describe the weather] - we keep thinking of you and wondering what it's like where you are.

We're keeping your room just as you left it. Everything is waiting for you.

Counting the days,
[Your Name]

title: Halfway There
body:
Dear [Recruit Name],

Can you believe it? You're past the halfway point. The finish line is real now - we can almost see it from here.

Look how far you've come since that first week. Every early morning, every hard run, every moment of doubt you pushed through - that was you. That was your strength.

We've been tracking every week on our calendar here at home. We cross off each day together, getting closer to the moment we see your face again.

The graduation date is circled. We already have our plans. We cannot wait to see you walk across that field.

Almost there,
[Your Name]

title: We'll Be There
body:
Dear [Recruit Name],

Graduation is close. We have our plans made, our bags ready, and our hearts full.

I've been thinking about the moment I'll see your face - standing in formation, in uniform, having done something extraordinary. I don't know if I'll be able to hold it together. I probably won't.

You set out to do something hard and you did it. You became something. And you did it while we loved you from a distance, which is the hardest kind of loving there is.

Come home proud. Come home rested when you can. And know that everything waiting for you here is better because of you.

We'll be there, cheering you on.

All our love,
[Your Name]
